assertions.expectation: Explains that Extended channel provides up to 24 months of support vs 14 months for standard channels
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Up to 24 months vs 14 months standard support' in the benefits section

assertions.expectation: Mentions that Extended channel has additional cost (it's not free)
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Extended channel is free during the standard 14-month support period' and 'Extra charges apply only during months 15-24 (the extended period)'

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended channel gets the same versions as Regular but keeps them available longer
assertions.reason: Response states 'Extended gets the same versions as Regular channel, same timing' and explains the 'Extended' aspect refers to support duration, not version arrival

assertions.expectation: Warns that staying on older versions longer means missing new features and accumulating technical debt
assertions.reason: The response does not mention missing new features or accumulating technical debt as consequences of staying on older versions longer

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel to Extended
assertions.reason: Response provides the exact command: 'gcloud container clusters update CLUSTER_NAME --region REGION --release-channel extended'

assertions.expectation: Advises checking that the current version (1.31) is available on Extended before switching
assertions.reason: Response includes a pre-migration assessment with command to verify 1.31 availability on Extended and confirms 'Extended supports 1.27+'

assertions.expectation: Recommends Extended for environments with compliance requirements or slow internal change processes
assertions.reason: Response recommends Extended for production clusters and states it's 'well-suited for teams that prefer deliberate, scheduled maintenance over automated upgrades'

assertions.expectation: Mentions that Extended doesn't mean you can skip upgrades forever — you still must upgrade within 24 months
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Must plan minor upgrades manually vs relying on auto-upgrade' and discusses processes to track when versions approach end-of-extended-support

assertions.expectation: Notes that minor version upgrades on Extended are NOT automated (except at EoS) — customers must plan and initiate them manually
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Minor version upgrades on Extended are NOT automated (except at end of extended support)' and 'Your team must plan and initiate minor upgrades proactively'
